title: Security Roles and Responsibilities Assignment Completeness

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 12.5.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.88: Security Roles and Responsibilities Assignment Completeness.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.88' running on index=hr sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment" — role_name, primary_user, backup_user, effective_date, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=hr sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment" earliest=-1d
| where match(role_name,"(?i)CISO|PCI\\s*Lead|IR\\s*Lead|NetSec|DBA\\s*CHD)")
| eval missing_primary=isnull(primary_user) OR primary_user=""
| eval missing_backup=isnull(backup_user) OR backup_user=""
| where missing_primary=1 OR missing_backup=1
| table role_name, primary_user, backup_user, effective_date
| sort role_name
description: Verifies each PCI-defined security role in the responsibility matrix has a named primary and backup assignee in the HR or IAM feed so accountability for security activities is not ambiguous during audits.
value: Verifies each PCI-defined security role in the responsibility matrix has a named primary and backup assignee in the HR or IAM feed so accountability for security activities is not ambiguous during audits.
implementation: (1) Align `role_name` strings with PCI responsibility matrix. (2) Daily sync from Workday/Azure AD groups. (3) Page HRIS on gaps. (4) Quarterly executive sign-off stored in GRC.
visualization: Table (gaps), matrix visual (role x assignee), single value (open gaps).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We check each -defined security role in the responsibility matrix has a named primary and backup assignee in the HR or access control feed so accountability for security activities is not ambiguous during audits — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment"` HEC from IAM.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=hr` `sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment"` — `role_name`, `primary_user`, `backup_user`, `effective_date`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align `role_name` strings with PCI responsibility matrix. (2) Daily sync from Workday/Azure AD groups. (3) Page HRIS on gaps. (4) Quarterly executive sign-off stored in GRC.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hr sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment" earliest=-1d
| where match(role_name,"(?i)CISO|PCI\\s*Lead|IR\\s*Lead|NetSec|DBA\\s*CHD)")
| eval missing_primary=isnull(primary_user) OR primary_user=""
| eval missing_backup=isnull(backup_user) OR backup_user=""
| where missing_primary=1 OR missing_backup=1
| table role_name, primary_user, backup_user, effective_date
| sort role_name
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Roles and Responsibilities Assignment Completeness (PCI DSS Req 12.1.3, 12.5.1)** — Verifies each PCI-defined security role in the responsibility matrix has a named primary and backup assignee in the HR or IAM feed so accountability for security activities is not ambiguous during audits.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=hr` `sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment"` — `role_name`, `primary_user`, `backup_user`, `effective_date`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment"` HEC from IAM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hr; **sourcetype**: iam:pci_role_assignment. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hr, sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(role_name,"(?i)CISO|PCI\\s*Lead|IR\\s*Lead|NetSec|DBA\\s*CHD)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **missing_primary** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **missing_backup** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where missing_primary=1 OR missing_backup=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Security Roles and Responsibilities Assignment Completeness (PCI DSS Req 12.1.3, 12.5.1)**): table role_name, primary_user, backup_user, effective_date
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (gaps), matrix visual (role x assignee), single value (open gaps).
